React Beginner to Advanced Series — Part 8A
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Non-member ? click here to read.
Congratulations!
If you’ve been following this React series from the beginning, you’ve already learned how to build reusable Components, pass data using Props, manage changing information with State, and respond to user actions using Events.
At this point, your application can react to user interactions.
But here’s an important question.
Should every user always see the same interface?
Of course not.
Imagine opening Netflix.
If you’re logged in, you immediately see:

• Continue Watching

• Trending Movies

• Your Watchlist

• Recommendations
Now imagine you’re not logged in.
Instead of movies, Netflix shows:

• Sign In

• Create Account

• Subscription Plans
The application decides what to display based on your current situation.
